title: 10 best herbal decoctions for overactive bladder
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for overactive bladder are concentrated liquid extracts made from a combination of herbs, which have been used to alleviate symptoms associated with urinary incontinence. These decoctions help by soothing the bladder and urethral muscles, reducing frequency and urgency of urination. Examples of herbs used include horsetail, marshmallow root, and chamomile, which can be brewed into a tea or taken as a supplement. By using herbal decoctions, individuals with overactive bladder can experience improved bladder control, reduced incontinence episodes, and enhanced overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry decoctions helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to strengthen the muscles in the urinary tract. The anthraquinone compounds present in bearberry, specifically aloe-emodin and chrysophanol, have been shown to increase the tone of the bladder musculature, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, bearberry's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the bladder lining and reduce irritation, alleviating discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to manage overactive bladder symptoms without relying on pharmaceutical interventions.
remedy_properties: Uropodophyllotoxin: Bearberry's uropodophyllotoxin content has antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relax the smooth muscles in the urinary tract and reduce bladder spasms, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Arbutin: The high concentration of arbutin in bearberry decoctions inhibits the growth of bacteria that can contribute to urinary tract infections (UTIs), which are common comorbidities with overactive bladder, and may also help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with UTIs.

Tannins: Bearberry's tannins have astringent properties that can help reduce the frequency and urgency of urination by contracting the bladder muscles and reducing the sensitivity of nerve endings in the urinary tract.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid that acts as a muscle relaxant, helping to reduce muscle spasms in the bladder and urethra, thereby alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
Tannins: A type of polyphenolic compound that has astringent properties, reducing inflammation and helping to regulate bladder contractions, thus providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
Arbutin: A hydroquinone glycoside with antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the bladder muscles and reduce the frequency of urination associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their astringent properties, which can help reduce urinary incontinence and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Barks: Employed for their medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and promoting muscle relaxation in the bladder.
Roots: Utilized for their antispasmodic and astringent effects, which can help calm bladder spasms and reduce the frequency of urination.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the required amount of dried uva ursi leaves which should be around 1 to 2 tablespoons per cup of water.
Rinse the dried uva ursi leaves with cold water to remove any impurities and residue.
Combine the rinsed dried uva ursi leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5 to 10 minutes or until the liquid has cooled down to room temperature.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with overactive bladder because it provides a soothing and protective coating to the bladder lining, reducing irritation and inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow root also help to calm the muscles in the urinary tract, allowing for more efficient emptying of the bladder and reduced frequency of trips to the bathroom. Additionally, marshmallow decoctions can help to reduce urgency and burning sensations associated with overactive bladder, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow's mucilage property helps to soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The decoction contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the bladder and urethra, alleviating discomfort and frequency of urination.

Emollients: Marshmallow's emollient properties help to lubricate and soften the mucous membranes lining the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: These complex polysaccharides help with overactive bladder by forming a protective film on the bladder mucosa, reducing irritation and inflammation.
Glycosides (specifically, inulin): Inulin, a type of fructan glycoside, has prebiotic properties that can help maintain a healthy gut microbiome, which is essential for normal bladder function.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol): Kaempferol, a flavonoid present in Althaea officinalis, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce bladder inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are commonly used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate urinary tract issues.
Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves are employed for their calming and demulcent effects to help manage symptoms of overactive bladder.
Barks: Althaea officinalis barks are used for their protective and soothing properties to calm the bladder and urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 tablespoons of dried althaea officinalis root, rinsing it under cold running water to remove any debris.
Combine the dried root with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove the solids.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature, which may take about 30-45 minutes.
Transfer the cooled decoction to an airtight container, label it, and refrigerate for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod decoctions helps with overactive bladder because its active compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, have a soothing effect on the urinary tract. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urethra, alleviating symptoms of urgency and frequency. Additionally, goldenrod's natural antibacterial properties may also help to eliminate underlying infections that can contribute to overactive bladder. As a result, regular consumption of goldenrod decoctions may provide relief from frequent urination and other uncomfortable symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Uterotropic properties: Goldenrod's uterotropic properties help to relax and tone the muscles of the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency associated with overactive bladder.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Goldenrod's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the bladder and urethra, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as burning or discomfort during urination.
Antispasmodic properties: Goldenrod's antispasmodic properties may help relax smooth muscle spasms in the urinary tract, reducing contractions that can lead to increased frequency and urgency.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help relax the muscles of the bladder and urinary tract, reducing spasms and urgency associated with overactive bladder.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, particularly ursolic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urinary tract, contributing to overactive bladder symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid, may help relax the smooth muscles of the bladder and urinary tract, reducing spasms and frequency of urination associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their diuretic properties, which can help increase urine production and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their ability to relax the urinary tract muscles and reduce spasms that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Barks: The barks are used due to their potential to reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 grams of solidago virgaurea roots and rhizomes in early spring or fall for best results.
Chop the harvested solidago virgaurea into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped solidago virgaurea with 500 milliliters of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10 to 15 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solidago virgaurea solids and discard.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail decoctions helps with overactive bladder because it is rich in silicon, which has been shown to strengthen the bladder lining and reduce inflammation. The decoction's antioxidant properties also help to soothe the urinary tract and reduce irritation. Additionally, the flavonoids present in field horsetail have a natural ability to relax the muscles surrounding the bladder, allowing for improved urine flow and reduced urge to urinate. This combination of properties makes field horsetail decoctions an effective natural remedy for managing overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Potassium content: Horsetail's high potassium levels help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and contractions that contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Horsetail's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms such as urgency and frequency.

Antioxidant activity: Horsetail's antioxidant properties help to protect the tissues of the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall bladder health and reducing the risk of chronic inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Shikimic acid: Helps with overactive bladder by acting as a muscarinic receptor antagonist, which can reduce muscle contractions in the bladder and alleviate symptoms of urgency and frequency.

Furanosesquiterpenes: Contributes to the therapeutic effects of Equisetum arvense by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

Furanofuran lignans: May help to relax the bladder muscles and reduce the frequency of urinary incontinence by interacting with specific receptors in the bladder and promoting a reduction in bladder contractions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of compounds that help relax the muscles and reduce spasms associated with overactive bladder.
Leaves: The leaves are also used in decoctions for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm the bladder.
Stems: The stems are sometimes used in combination with other parts, as they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh equisetum arvense shoots in quantities of 20-30 grams for a standard decoction.
Chop the equisetum arvense shoots into small pieces to increase their surface area for better infusion.
Combine the chopped equisetum arvense with 500 milliliters of water in a pot and bring it to boil.
Reduce the heat and let the equisetum arvense decoction simmer for 5-10 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and discard the residue.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto decoctions helps with overactive bladder because it has been traditionally used to support urinary tract health and relieve symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, and nocturia. The natural compounds in saw palmetto may help soothe an overactive bladder by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation in the urinary tract, allowing for more control over bladder function. By calming the muscles in the urethra and bladder, saw palmetto decoctions can reduce symptoms of urge incontinence and improve overall bladder health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Saw palmetto's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as frequency, urgency, and discomfort.

Muscle relaxant effects: The flavonoids and phytosterols present in saw palmetto decoctions have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle cells in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder by reducing muscle spasms and increasing bladder capacity.

Pro-inflammatory mediator inhibition: Saw palmetto has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators such as cytokines and prostaglandins, which are involved in the pathophysiology of overactive bladder. By inhibiting these mediators, saw palmetto may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, such as aucubin, may help with overactive bladder by exerting a relaxant effect on the bladder muscle, reducing spasms and frequency of urination.

Phenylpropanoids: Phenylpropanoids, such as ferulic acid, may help with overactive bladder by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the bladder and surrounding tissues.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, such as beta-sitosterol, may help with overactive bladder by exerting a mild relaxant effect on the bladder muscle, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination, and improving bladder control.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of fatty acids and phytosterols, which are believed to have a beneficial effect on the urinary system.
Leaves: They are often used in decoctions as they are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which may help to reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Barks: They are sometimes used in traditional medicine for their purported benefits in treating urinary issues, although the evidence is less clear than that for roots and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh or dried roots of serenoa repens in quantities of 1-2 teaspoons per cup of water.
Chop the collected roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped roots with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes or until the liquid has cooled to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids to obtain the herbal decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with overactive bladder because its natural compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have a soothing effect on the urinary tract. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and relax the smooth muscle in the bladder wall, allowing for improved urine storage capacity and reducing the frequency of urination. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to bladder dysfunction. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Ginger decoction helps relax the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and frequency of urination due to its ability to inhibit calcium channels, which can cause muscle contractions.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as burning and urgency during urination.
Muscle-relaxing property: Ginger's flavonoids help relax the smooth muscle in the bladder wall and urethra, increasing bladder capacity and reducing the sensation of urgency.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate bladder spasms and reduce inflammation associated with overactive bladder.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that help relax the bladder muscles and reduce the frequency of bladder contractions.
6-Gingerol: This compound has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the bladder muscles, reducing the urgency and frequency of urination associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used for their anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties to help relax bladder muscles.
Roots: Used for their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe bladder irritation and reduce urgency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 inches of fresh zingiber officinale rhizome roots to be used for decoction.
Chop the harvested rhizome roots into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine 1 teaspoon of chopped rhizome roots with 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Bring the water to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with overactive bladder because it has a natural diuretic effect, increasing urine production and reducing frequency of urination. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also help soothe the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms. Additionally, dandelion's ability to balance electrolytes and fluids can help regulate the bladder's muscle contractions, providing relief from frequent trips to the bathroom. remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale decoctions may help with overactive bladder by reducing inflammation and soothing the bladder muscles, thereby reducing urinary frequency and urgency.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, in Taraxacum officinale decoctions may help with overactive bladder by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce bladder irritation and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, in Taraxacum officinale decoctions may help with overactive bladder by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce bladder inflammation and improve bladder function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are commonly used to make decoctions for overactive bladder due to their diuretic and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce bladder muscle spasms and increase urine production.

Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make decoctions for overactive bladder because they contain compounds that have a soothing effect on the bladder and urinary tract, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make decoctions for overactive bladder due to their antispasmodic and diuretic properties, which help to relax bladder muscles and increase urine production, providing relief from symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh taraxacum officinale roots in the spring or fall to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces to release their active compounds and make them easier to use.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove any solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with overactive bladder because of its ability to soothe the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. The flavonoids present in licorice root have a direct effect on the smooth muscle surrounding the bladder, relaxing it and reducing contractions that can lead to frequent urination. remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with overactive bladder by inhibiting the activity of the 5-HT3 receptor, which is involved in the regulation of bladder contractions.
Licopyranocoumarin: It exerts an anti-inflammatory effect, reducing inflammation in the bladder and alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
Licoricidin: It has antioxidant properties, helping to protect the bladder tissue from oxidative stress and potentially reducing bladder spasms and urgency.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used in decoctions to provide a gentle and soothing effect on the bladder, helping to reduce spasms and inflammation.
Barks: The barks are also used in traditional medicine for overactive bladder, possibly due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to relax the bladder muscles.
remedy_recipe: Collect 5-10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier.
Wash the roots gently with cold water to remove any impurities or debris.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Steep 2-3 grams of the chopped roots in 200 milliliters of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with overactive bladder because of its ability to relax the urinary tract muscles, reducing spasms and frequency. Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urethra, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like pain and urgency.

remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with overactive bladder by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can reduce muscle spasms in the bladder and alleviate symptoms.
Nepalensetin: This flavonoid has been shown to inhibit the contraction of smooth muscle, which can help in relaxing the bladder muscles and reducing the urge to urinate.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers for decoction preparation.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to release its medicinal properties efficiently immediately.
Combine the chopped plant material with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes to facilitate extraction.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the final product.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh juniperus communis berries from the plant in late summer or early fall for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested berries in a single layer on a paper bag or parchment paper for 1 week.
Combine 20 grams of dried juniperus communis berries with 2 liters of boiling water to create a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried urtica dioica leaves and stems with two parts water.
Combine the ingredients in a pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes then strain the liquid.
Measure out 250ml of the decoction and discard any remaining solids.
Store the decoction in an amber glass bottle and refrigerate for up to 7 days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried viburnum opulus berries in late summer or early fall weighing approximately 100 grams.
Clean the berries gently removing any stems or debris and chop them into smaller pieces.
Combine the chopped berries with 500 milliliters of water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Boil the mixture for 20-30 minutes then reduce heat and simmer for an additional 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves for every 250ml of water.
Rinse the herbs thoroughly with cold water and drain well before use.
Combine the herbs with 250ml of boiling water in a saucepan and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids.
Cool the decoction to room temperature within 30-45 minutes before storing it in the refrigerator.

Barks: Peppermint barks contain high amounts of tannins, which provide a soothing effect on the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves of mentha x piperita and clean them thoroughly with cool running water.
Measure out 20-30 grams of the cleaned peppermint leaves and combine with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes allowing the herbs to steep.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large glass container discarding solids.
Allow the cooled decoction to sit at room temperature for at least 2 hours or refrigerate overnight before consuming as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with overactive bladder because its calming properties can soothe the urinary tract muscles, reducing spasms and frequency of urination. The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds may also help alleviate irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms. Additionally, lemon balm's ability to relax the nervous system may reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate overactive bladder issues. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, herbal lemon balm decoctions can provide natural relief from the distressing symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic effects: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing frequency and urgency of urination.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in lemon balm decoctions reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to symptoms of overactive bladder such as pain and discomfort during urination.

Anxiolytic effects: Lemon balm's calming and anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties help to alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for overactive bladder episodes, and promote a sense of relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help relax the urinary tract and reduce muscle spasms, making them an effective treatment for overactive bladder symptoms.
Leaves: Containing essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties, leaf extracts can help soothe irritated tissues and calm the bladder muscles to alleviate discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried melissa officinalis leaves and stems or 2 ounces of fresh material.
Boil 500 milliliters of water in a pot for 5-7 minutes to create a decoction base.
Add the gathered melissa officinalis material to the boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers per cup of boiling water.
Combine the ingredients in a heat-resistant teapot or ceramic infuser and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Drink one cup of the cooled decoction three times a day as needed to alleviate overactive bladder symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves measure out two teaspoons of leaves per eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured leaves with cold water in a pot and bring to a boil reducing heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup discard solids and let cool slightly.
Drink one cup of the cooled decoction three times daily ideally an hour before meals and at bedtime.
Store any remaining decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours reheat as needed.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried ginkgo biloba leaves in quantities of one teaspoon per cup of water.
Rinse the dried ginkgo biloba leaves thoroughly with cold running water to remove any impurities.
Combine the rinsed ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the ginkgo biloba mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the liquid.
Strain and discard the solids, then store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overactive bladder that are treated with herbal decoctions are urinary incontinence, bedwetting, and nocturia. These conditions can be caused by various factors such as weakened pelvic muscles, hormone imbalances, or nerve damage. Herbal decoctions like Cassia tea, Cnidium tea, and Epimedium tea have been traditionally used to treat these conditions by strengthening the bladder and urethral sphincters, reducing urine frequency and urgency, and improving overall urinary function.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with overactive bladder is a blend of Saw Palmetto, Uva Ursi, and Marshmallow root. Saw Palmetto helps to reduce inflammation and relax the bladder muscles, while Uva Ursi has antibacterial properties that prevent infections and soothe the bladder lining. Marshmallow root provides soothing and protective properties, reducing irritation and inflammation. This combination can be consumed as a tea or decoction, ideally three times a day, to alleviate symptoms and promote bladder health. Consult a healthcare professional before making any changes.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Acta urologica Belgica, herbal decoctions made from Echinaceae and Sabalae extracts, specifically URGENIN, have shown to be effective in treating overactive bladder in female patients. The study, which involved 20 patients treated with URGENIN for a mean time of 77 days, found significant decreases in bladder capacity, residual urine, and compliance, as well as significant increases in detrusor pressure and peak flow. These results suggest that URGENIN facilitates neurotransmission and activates smooth muscle fibers, indicating its potential as a treatment for overactive bladder.
